senses.definition: to serve as the reason for a particular occurrence or outcome

examples.x: A sudden increase in demand for electric cars can account for the rising prices of lithium.
The stormy weather can account for the delay in our flight.
Lack of proper communication may account for the team's mismanagement of the project.

senses.definition: to cause something to change

examples.x: New technologies can greatly alter the way we live and communicate.
After receiving feedback, she decided to alter her presentation to make it more engaging.
A good haircut can completely alter one's appearance.
Last year, the company altered its marketing strategy to reach a wider audience.
The architect altered the design after receiving feedback from the client.

senses.definition: to begin to exist or become noticeable

examples.x: Unexpected challenges can arise during the course of a project, requiring swift problem-solving.
It was only when the sun set that the need for additional lighting arose in the outdoor event.
Tensions began to arise among team members due to differing opinions on the project's direction.
A sense of urgency arose when the company realized the impending deadline for product launch.

senses.definition: to increase or improve the progress, growth, or success of something

examples.x: Regular exercise can boost your overall health and well-being.
The government plans to boost the economy by investing in infrastructure.
Adding a variety of nutrients to your diet can boost your immune system.
The recent upgrade to the software will boost its performance and reliability.
She took a course to boost her skills and advance her career in graphic design.

senses.definition: to become more powerful, intense, or larger in quantity

examples.x: As the tension builds up, everyone anticipates the final match.
Confidence tends to build up when you achieve small goals.
Pressure can build up in a closed system if not released properly.
The excitement is starting to build up as the concert date approaches.
Over time, clutter can build up in the attic if not addressed.

senses.definition: to increase in terms of amount, value, intensity, etc.

examples.x: With successful marketing strategies, the sales of the product began to climb steadily.
As the economy improved, employment rates started to climb.
The athlete's training regimen needed to climb to a higher level to stay at the top of their game.
With the growing demand for online services, internet usage began to climb significantly.

senses.definition: to be one of the causes or reasons that helps something happen

examples.x: Many factors contribute to the rise in housing prices.
The feedback from customers contributed to improving the product.
Volunteer efforts significantly contribute to the charity's growth.
His hard work contributed to the project's success.
Her insights contributed to the development of the innovative idea.

senses.definition: someone or something's role in achieving a specific result, particularly a positive one

examples.x: Employees are rewarded based on their individual contributions to the company's success.
Her many years of service and contributions have helped make our organization what it is today.
Students are assessed on the contributions they make to classroom discussions and projects.

senses.definition: to reduce in amount, size, intensity, etc.

examples.x: Sales often decline during economic downturns.
With the changing weather, the temperature tends to decline in the winter months.
The popularity of the product declined after the release of a newer version.
Demand was declining as the market shifted toward alternative products.
Morale among the employees was declining during the restructuring period.

senses.definition: to better or increase someone or something's quality, strength, value, etc.

examples.x: Adding new features can enhance the functionality of a software application.
Regular exercise can enhance physical fitness and overall health.
The chef used special spices to enhance the flavor of the dish.
Training programs are designed to enhance employees' skills and performance.
Educational programs aim to enhance students' knowledge and learning experiences.

senses.definition: to enlarge or lengthen something

examples.x: She extends her hand for a friendly handshake.
The construction workers are extending the road to improve traffic flow.
The company has extended its product line to attract a wider customer base.
The artist was extending the canvas to create a larger painting.
The city council plans to extend the park by adding more green space.

senses.definition: to extend or move something in one direction without any bends or curves

examples.x: She straightened the ribbon, carefully pulling it taut to create a neat and uniform bow.
The farmer straightened the fence posts, aligning them in a perfect row along the field boundary.
The teacher straightened the rows of desks in the classroom, creating an orderly and conducive learning environment.
She straightened the hose before watering the garden, ensuring the water flowed smoothly without obstruction.
The electrician straightened the cable before securing it to the wall, ensuring a clean and professional installation.

senses.definition: (of currencies, prices, etc.) to increase in value

examples.x: The stock market is expected to gain this quarter.
Prices of essential goods have begun to gain due to demand.
His investments gained significantly over the year.
The real estate market continues to gain in value.
She noticed that her savings gained interest over time.

senses.definition: (particularly of a price, rate, etc.) to increase sharply

examples.x: After the company announced record profits, the stock prices jumped significantly in just one day.
Following the release of the highly anticipated product, consumer demand caused sales to jump by 30%.
The interest rates on loans jumped sharply as a response to economic uncertainties.
With the sudden surge in demand, housing prices in the city jumped by 15% in a matter of weeks.

senses.definition: to decrease in degree, amount, quality, or strength

examples.x: After the rainfall, the river's water level began to lower gradually.
The temperature outside is gradually lowering as evening approaches.
With each passing hour, her energy levels began to lower, signaling the need for rest.
The intensity of the argument began to lower as both parties started to calm down.

senses.definition: to make minor changes to something so that it is more suitable or better

examples.x: The tailor often modifies dresses to ensure a perfect fit.
Engineers need to modify the design slightly to enhance the performance of the machine.
The architect proposed to modify the building layout to improve its functionality.
The software developer must modify the code to address a minor bug in the program.
The teacher modified the lesson plan and saw positive results in student engagement.

senses.definition: to gradually rise or increase

examples.x: As tensions escalated, fears of a conflict began to mount in the international community.
With each passing day, excitement started to mount as the anticipated event drew closer.
Amid the economic uncertainty, the unemployment rate started to mount, affecting various industries.
With persistent efforts, the company saw its profits gradually mount over the fiscal year.
The evidence against the suspect continued to mount, making a compelling case for the prosecution.

senses.definition: to significantly increase in quantity

examples.x: If you multiply your efforts, you will see better results.
The team's success can multiply their chances of winning the championship.
The organization aims to multiply its impact by collaborating with others.
When conditions are favorable, crops can multiply quickly.

senses.definition: to become normal again after a period of difficulty

examples.x: The economy is slowly starting to recover after the recession.
The community worked together to recover after the natural disaster.
The company is slowly recovering after the financial crisis.
The economy is expected to recover in the next few years.
It’s been a tough year, but they are starting to recover.

senses.definition: to cause something to happen

examples.x: The economic downturn triggered a series of layoffs within the company.
The unexpected news about the merger triggered a surge in stock prices.
A shortage of raw materials can trigger delays in the production schedule.
A sudden change in weather conditions can trigger a cascade of flight cancellations at airports.
The controversial decision by the government triggered widespread protests across the nation.

senses.definition: an increase in the amount, size, importance, or degree of something

examples.x: The expansion of the business into international markets was successful.
The expansion of the highway will reduce traffic congestion.
The organization announced plans for the expansion of its services to underserved communities.
The city is undergoing rapid expansion with new housing developments.
The expansion of the company led to new job opportunities in the region.

senses.definition: a possible consequence that something can bring about

examples.x: His decision to cut costs has serious implications for employee morale.
The implications of the new law could affect many small businesses.
The report highlighted the implications of climate change for future generations.
She understood the implications of her choice to move to a new city.

senses.definition: a way, system, object, etc. through which one can achieve a goal or accomplish a task

examples.x: Education is a powerful means to improve one's future prospects.
Hard work and determination are essential means to achieve success.
Public transportation provides a means for many people to commute to work.
Saving and investing wisely are effective means to secure financial stability.
Art can be a means of expressing complex emotions and ideas.

senses.definition: the result or consequence of a situation, event, or action

examples.x: The outcome of the election was surprising to many analysts.
Studying hard typically leads to a positive outcome on exams.
We are eagerly waiting to see the outcome of the new product launch.
Proper planning and execution can greatly influence the outcome of any endeavor.
Market trends can often predict the outcome of business investments.

senses.definition: a thing or person resulted from something particular

examples.x: His success is a product of years of hard work and dedication.
Her fluency in multiple languages is a product of living in different countries throughout her childhood.
The conflict was a product of long-standing tensions between the two communities.
The current economic downturn is a product of several global factors.

senses.definition: a decline in amount, degree, etc. of a particular thing

examples.x: The company implemented cost-cutting measures that led to a reduction in expenses.
Regular exercise and a healthy diet can lead to a reduction in cholesterol levels.
The reduction in traffic congestion was noticeable after the new highway was opened.
The government announced a reduction in taxes to stimulate economic growth.
The reduction in greenhouse gas emissions is crucial for combating climate change.

senses.definition: the primary cause of something

examples.x: Understanding the root of the problem is essential for finding an effective solution to the conflict.
The therapist helped her uncover the root of her anxiety, which was tied to past experiences.
Finding the root of the misunderstanding allowed them to resolve the issue and improve communication.
The company conducted a thorough analysis to determine the root of the financial problems affecting their performance.

senses.definition: being the main cause of something

examples.x: She felt responsible for the project's delays due to her oversight.
The heavy rain was responsible for the flooding in the area.
The faulty wiring was found to be responsible for the fire.

senses.definition: used to indicate a logical result or effect

examples.x: The team neglected to conduct thorough testing, and consequently, several critical errors emerged in the final product.
She ignored the warning signs of burnout, and consequently, her overall well-being suffered.
The team neglected to conduct thorough testing, and consequently, several critical errors emerged in the final product.
The project timeline was poorly managed, and consequently, the team struggled to meet crucial milestones.
The company invested heavily in research and development, and consequently, they launched innovative products that captured a wider market share.

senses.definition: in a manner that is gradually growing in degree, extent, or frequency over time

examples.x: The company is increasingly investing in sustainable practices.
With each passing day, the weather is getting increasingly colder.
The team is increasingly confident about achieving its goals.
The use of renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly popular.
The project's complexity is increasingly challenging, requiring more resources.

senses.definition: so impactful that can change someone's life

examples.x: The workshop was a life-changing experience for many attendees.
Moving to a new city can be a life-changing opportunity for personal growth.
Reading that book had a life-changing effect on his outlook on life.
Attending that conference turned out to be a life-changing experience for her.

senses.definition: important or great enough to be noticed or have an impact

examples.x: The discovery of the ancient artifact was significant for understanding the region's history.
His contributions to the project were significant in achieving its success.
The new policy had a significant impact on improving workplace productivity.
The student's research findings were significant enough to be published in a prestigious journal.
The company's decision to expand into international markets was significant for its growth strategy.

senses.definition: to a noticeable or considerable extent

examples.x: The company's revenue has grown significantly since last quarter.
Her health has improved significantly after the new treatment.
Test scores rose significantly across all age groups.
The road conditions have worsened significantly due to the storm.
He contributed significantly to the success of the project.

senses.definition: used to indicate what happens as a result of something
used to say that one thing is a result of another

examples.x: She missed the bus, hence she arrived late to the meeting.
The flight was delayed, hence they had to reschedule their connecting flight.
The store was closed, hence they had to find another place to shop.
The weather forecast predicted a snowstorm; hence, schools were closed for the day.
The company invested in employee training programs; hence, the overall performance and efficiency improved.

senses.definition: used to introduce a result based on the information or actions that came before

examples.x: She saved consistently each month; thus, she was able to afford the vacation she had always dreamed of.
He missed the last train; thus, he had to find an alternative mode of transportation to reach his destination.
The proposal received unanimous approval from the board; thus, the project was officially greenlit.
The market demand for renewable energy sources is increasing; thus, more companies are investing in sustainable technologies.
The new software significantly improved efficiency; thus, the company experienced a notable increase in productivity.

senses.definition: to fill someone with the desire or motivation to do something, especially something creative or positive

examples.x: Her teacher's words of encouragement inspired her to pursue her dreams.
The artist's masterpiece inspires others to explore their own creativity.
The coach's motivational speeches inspired the team to achieve victory.
Reading about successful entrepreneurs inspires her to start her own business.
The leader's vision and determination inspired the team to overcome challenges.

senses.definition: the initial stage or place in which a significant event or activity happens or starts
